title: Invalid PEP 517 build backend in pyproject.toml
evidence: pyproject.toml:3 build-backend = "setuptools.backends.legacy:build" — no such module; the real backend is "setuptools.build_meta". `pip install .` / `python -m build` would fail at backend import.

title: Console-script entry point points to nonexistent server:main
evidence: pyproject.toml:17 [project.scripts] brazilian-soccer-mcp = "server:main" but server.py has no main(); it uses `if __name__ == "__main__"` (server.py:177). Installed `brazilian-soccer-mcp` command would ImportError.
suggestion: Add a main() wrapper in server.py or point the script at an existing callable.

title: Match filtering exposes season but not a date range
evidence: query_engine.py:99-104 handles season/Ano only; no start/end date filter. R4 asks for 'date range and/or season' — the 'or season' branch is satisfied and tested (test_query_engine.py:39), date-range is absent.
suggestion: Optionally accept from_date/to_date and filter on date_parsed.

title: Unfiltered aggregate stats double-count overlapping datasets
evidence: query_engine.py:305,312,319 aggregate over all five match DataFrames; br_football (10,296 rows) overlaps brasileirao/historical, so get_average_goals()/get_home_win_rate() without a competition filter count the same matches multiple times.
suggestion: Deduplicate across sources or default aggregates to a single competition.

title: Unknown competition string silently yields empty results
evidence: query_engine.py:43-46 filters datasets by substring; an unrecognized competition (e.g. 'seriea') matches nothing and returns [] with no error, so callers can't distinguish 'no matches' from 'bad filter'.
suggestion: Validate competition against the known set and raise/return a clear error.

title: Team stats merge same-named clubs across states
evidence: query_engine.py:161 matches team by normalized substring, so get_team_stats('Atletico') aggregates Atlético-MG/PR/GO together; standings (query_engine.py:263) deliberately use raw names but team-stats/head-to-head do not.
suggestion: Offer an exact/raw-name mode for team stats and head-to-head consistency.
